Started a school discussion group on farming problems and local solutions.
Earned small income through tutoring and produce-related help.
annotations.review_notes: Strong rural systems thinking, reflection, and repeated responsibility. Still support-needing, but clearly committee-relevant.

evidence_bullets: Tested homemade drip and compost methods on family crop rows.
Helped build a printed shelf and discussion point for farming materials.
Video and attachment evidence make the village-scale work more legible.
annotations.review_notes: Same underlying strength as other rural high-potential cases, with better direct evidence despite modest presentation.

evidence_bullets: Recovered from academic setback and now tutors a peer in math.
Created a library feedback wall and attempted a coding club.
Built simple websites for teachers for small fees or free.
annotations.review_notes: Growth trajectory is still the core signal. Evidence is more modest than the stronger variant, but the upward movement is real.

evidence_bullets: Turned academic recovery into peer tutoring and school initiatives.
Built teacher websites and library feedback tools with visible outputs.
Interview, video, and documents make the change arc concrete.
annotations.review_notes: Strongest version of this growth-candidate family. Concrete actions across channels justify high confidence.

evidence_bullets: Built a power bank from old batteries and repaired local devices.
Helped restore a school library computer with classmates.
Resold a repaired laptop after hardware and system work.
annotations.review_notes: Technical agency is real, but documentation is lighter than in the stronger multimodal variant.

evidence_bullets: Kept repair logs, photos, and parts records for real hardware projects.
Organized a three-person library-computer repair effort.
Restored and sold old laptops after component replacement and testing.
annotations.review_notes: Very strong practical engineering evidence with repeat execution and visible ownership. Still understated relative to capability.

evidence_bullets: Ran a school clothing collection and helped with a shelter fundraiser.
Earned money through tutoring and courier work.
Reflects on volunteer work without inflated claims.
annotations.review_notes: Promising socially useful pattern with thinner evidence than the stronger version. Support and structure could raise this profile.

evidence_bullets: Organized a clothing drive with sorting and delivery logistics.
Ran a school fundraiser for an animal shelter and tracked budget details.
Balanced paid work with tutoring and community action.
annotations.review_notes: Concrete community execution and stronger documentation move this into clear review priority.

evidence_bullets: Ran a small girls speaking-confidence session with follow-up.
Tutored younger students and reflects on what actually changed.
Explicitly distinguishes good atmosphere from real impact.
annotations.review_notes: More grounded than the polished source profile. Scale remains limited, but authenticity looks much stronger.

evidence_bullets: Describes motivational meetings in broad terms without concrete outcomes.
Uses abstract value language instead of operational detail.
Shows no strong example of sustained follow-through.
annotations.review_notes: Useful negative-control profile for polish-vs-evidence testing. The issue is generic, lightly grounded evidence, not proof of cheating.

evidence_bullets: Started a reading circle for children in the apartment complex.
Helped organize a neighborhood clothing-swap event.
Later tutored younger students based on trust from the club.
annotations.review_notes: Grassroots initiative is visible even with lighter records. Scale is modest, so evidence strength stays moderate.

evidence_bullets: Kept attendance and parent coordination for a repeated reading club.
Helped run a clothing swap and learned from logistics failures.
Converted community trust into paid tutoring with lesson planning.
annotations.review_notes: Strong neighborhood organizer profile with multimodal proof and good reflection on infrastructure, not just goodwill.

evidence_bullets: Ran repeated speaking-practice sessions for anxious classmates.
Adjusted format based on participant needs and feedback.
Tutored younger students and links communication to concrete usefulness.
annotations.review_notes: Communication is now backed by repeat facilitation and bounded claims. Much less polish-driven than the source profile.

evidence_bullets: Built a short workshop format for students afraid of public speaking.
Moved from generic encouragement to repeated peer practice.
Video transcript shows actual outline and exercises rather than slogans.
annotations.review_notes: Still communication-heavy, but the profile has enough practical scaffolding to treat it as real execution.

evidence_bullets: Built a recurring Saturday repair club with intake notes and tool sharing.
Repairs devices, resells restored phones, and reinvests into tools.
Teaches younger students basic safety and repair limits.
annotations.review_notes: Strong builder-founder signal with repeat ownership, systems thinking, and sustainable local value creation.

evidence_bullets: Created a WhatsApp and paper order system for family produce sales.
Improved routing, stock tracking, and repeat-customer reliability.
Helped another nearby family adapt the same process.
annotations.review_notes: Plain self-presentation hides meaningful operations thinking. This is one of the strongest new hidden-potential business-process cases.

evidence_bullets: Completed a water-hardness project and assisted in lab sessions.
Reflects on contamination error and tighter process control.
Motivation for the program remains externally framed and thin.
annotations.review_notes: Evidence is stronger than the story. Keep motivation authenticity lower without collapsing the applicant's technical capability.

evidence_bullets: Built equipment checklists and handled technical setup for events.
Earned repeat paid work through reliable backstage execution.
Motivation is practical but weakly connected to the program mission.
annotations.review_notes: Clear execution competence with limited intrinsic program pull. Good contrast case for evidence-vs-motivation separation.

evidence_bullets: Built a small oral-history archive with recording and editing workflow.
Coordinated peers on documentary audio tasks and consent-aware practice.
Includes portfolio-like proof and real outside-school use.
annotations.review_notes: Strong arts-first execution case with disciplined method and civic value, not just creative language.

evidence_bullets: Performs repeated household and small-shop electrical maintenance.
Created a repair checklist and organized tools and materials.
Paid work and video evidence show reliability and safety awareness.
annotations.review_notes: Practical execution and responsibility are stronger than the profile's polish. High-potential vocational case that likely needs academic support.

evidence_bullets: Started a small theatre circle for shy younger students.
Uses simple exercises and progression rather than high-gloss performance.
Balanced facilitation with caregiving pressure at home.
annotations.review_notes: Support-needing but promising arts facilitator. Evidence is modest, yet the interpersonal leadership signal is real.

evidence_bullets: Uses polished policy language with little concrete execution detail.
Main example is a small discussion gathering with unclear outcomes.
Interview admits the experience is exploratory rather than deep.
annotations.review_notes: Strong negative-control case for over-polished, evidence-light applications. The review flag is about generic grounding, not proof of cheating.

evidence_bullets: Relies on branding and influence language more than factual actions.
Examples stay at the level of school atmosphere and vague media ideas.
No durable outcomes or concrete ownership are shown.
annotations.review_notes: Another polish-heavy low-evidence control. Weak evidence should drive the score more than style should lift it.

evidence_bullets: Built care schedules, reminders, and escort coordination for older adults.
Adapted the system for another family in the building.
Shows reliable logistics, not just family-duty narrative.
annotations.review_notes: Strong hidden-potential case where care work translates into real coordination skill. Clearly support-needing but valuable.

evidence_bullets: Balanced cafe shifts with self-taught Python and workflow improvements.
Built a shift-tracking tool to reduce scheduling confusion.
Earned income through service work and small digital help tasks.
annotations.review_notes: High-potential support-needing builder with clear growth arc. Modest polish should not hide the systems-thinking signal.

evidence_bullets: Built an accessibility map with route audits, photos, and user comments.
Revised categories after feedback and worked with peers on documentation.
Multimodal proof ties civic-design claims to concrete field observation.
annotations.review_notes: One of the strongest new multimodal civic-design cases. Evidence is rich, grounded, and committee-relevant.
